# Profile

I am Professor of Urban Analytics at the Bartlett Centre for Advanced Spatial
Analysis (CASA), University College London. My research uses data and
quantitative methods — frequently in R — to understand cities, migration,
housing and the systems people create. I publish across migration modelling
(particularly spatial interaction models), housing and energy, gentrification,
synthetic data and the geographies of beer and brewing, and have a passion
for applied work in urban policy contexts.

# Academic Staff Line Management

Currently line-manage 2 academic staff. As Head of Department over a 5-year
period directly line-managed (seeing several through promotions):

- 3 Professors (one successfully re-banded during period)
- 6 Associate Professors (2 promoted to Professor during period)
- 4 Lecturers (2 promoted to Associate Professor during period)
